name: NHS England Regional Secure Data Environment Network
description: A network of 11 regional Secure Data Environments across England, each covering a specific geographic area and providing secure access to local and regional health data. Regional SDEs complement the national SDE by offering access to local clinical datasets and closer relationships with clinical teams. Each regional SDE has its own data holdings, processes, and governance arrangements.
tre_platform: Regional Secure Data Environments (11 separate platforms across England)

requirements.name: Regional SDE application
requirements.description: Submit a data access application to the specific regional SDE covering the area of interest

requirements.name: Local governance approval
requirements.description: Each regional SDE has its own governance processes and review arrangements

requirements.name: SDE access agreement
requirements.description: Formal agreement with the regional SDE governing data access and use

access_steps.name: Identify appropriate regional SDE
access_steps.description: Determine which of the 11 regional SDEs covers the geographic area and data required for the study

access_steps.name: Submit application
access_steps.description: Complete and submit the data access application to the identified regional SDE

access_steps.name: Governance review
access_steps.description: Regional SDE governance review of the application (process varies by SDE)

access_steps.name: SDE provisioning
access_steps.description: Regional SDE workspace set up and data provisioned for the approved project

timeline.notes: Identifying SDE, scoping data availability, preparing application
SDE workspace setup and data provisioning

costs.tre_fee: Varies by SDE - generally free for approved research
costs.notes: Costs vary across the 11 regional SDEs. Most are free for approved academic research. Some may charge for industry access or complex data preparations.

strengths: Access to local and regional clinical data not available nationally
Closer working relationship with local clinical teams and NHS trusts
Potentially faster for local or regional studies
All 11 regional SDEs now operational across England
Part of the SDE network with potential for federated analysis
limitations: Varying maturity levels across the 11 regional SDEs
Inconsistent processes, governance, and data holdings across regions
No single entry point - researchers must identify the correct regional SDE
Some regional SDEs still building data holdings and infrastructure
recent_changes: Growing data holdings as more local datasets are onboarded
Increasing alignment of processes across the SDE network
Federated analysis capabilities being developed across SDE network
